---
title: R/broman User Guide
output:
    html_document:
        toc: true
        toc_float: true
---

```{r knitr_options, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(fig.width=7, fig.height=4.5)
old_digits <- options("digits")$digits
options(digits=3)
library(broman)
```

<link rel=stylesheet type="text/css" href="https://kbroman.org/stylesheets/kbroman_vignette.css"></script>

<a href="https://github.com/kbroman/broman"><img src="https://kbroman.org/broman/broman_logo.png" align="right" height="138" alt="R/broman logo"/></a>

[![R-CMD-check](https://github.com/kbroman/broman/actions/workflows/R-CMD-check.yaml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/kbroman/broman/actions/workflows/R-CMD-check.yaml)
[![CRAN_Status_Badge](https://www.r-pkg.org/badges/version/broman)](https://cran.r-project.org/package=broman)
[![r-universe badge](https://kbroman.r-universe.dev/broman/badges/version)](https://kbroman.r-universe.dev/broman)
[![zenodo DOI](https://zenodo.org/badge/DOI/10.5281/zenodo.2636796.svg)](https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.2636796)

[R/broman](https://github.com/kbroman/broman) is an
[R](https://www.r-project.org) package with miscellanoues R functions
that are useful to me. It's gotten rather large, and the functions do
a lot of different, unrelated things, so my purpose here is to
organize them and illustrate their use.


### Document helpers

There are a bunch of functions to help while writing data analysis reports.

- [`add_commas`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/broman/refman/broman.html#add_commas) -
  This adds commas to large numbers so they look more natural, so if
  `x` is 150000, `add_commas(x)` becomes `r add_commas(150000)`.


- [`myround`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/broman/refman/broman.html#myround) -
  I'm very particular about sig figs, so I may want `myround(3.10, 2)`
  to appear as `r myround(3.10, 2)` and not `r round(3.10, 2)`.

- [`spell_out`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/broman/refman/broman.html#spell_out) -
  Spell out an integer as a word; values <10 become words and values
  >=10 remain in numerals. So if `x` is 5, `spell_out(x)` becomes
  "five" while if it were 10 it would remain "10".

- [`numbers`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/broman/refman/broman.html#numbers) -
  This is just a vector of the first twenty positive integers as
  words, for a similar use as `spell_out()`.

- [`vec2string`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/broman/refman/broman.html#vec2string) -
  Take a vector of numbers and turn it into text, like
  `add_commas[problem_ids]` becomes `123, 125, 128, and 129`.

- [`kbdate`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/broman/refman/broman.html#kbdate`) -
  Shows today's date in a particular format. It seems unnecessary. I
  had used it for date stamps, but it seems to give the same output as `Sys.Date()`.


### Statistics stuff

- [`chisq`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/broman/refman/broman.html#chisq) -
  This is like `chisq.test()` but using Monte Carlo to get an
  approximate p-value rather than relying on asymptotics.

- [`fisher`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/broman/refman/broman.html#fisher) -
  This is like `fisher.test()` in doing Fisher's exact test but
  getting the p-value by Monte Carlo rather than doing an exhaustive calculation.

- [`perm.test`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/broman/refman/broman.html#perm.test) -
  This does a two-sample permutation test using the t statistic (via
  `t.test()`), using Monte Carlo to get the p-value rather than
  referring to the t distribution.

- [`paired.perm.test`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/broman/refman/broman.html#paired.perm.test)
  This is similar to `perm.test` but for paired data.

- [`quantileSE`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/broman/refman/broman.html#quantileSE) -
  This is like the function `quantile()` but it also gives an
  estimated standard error.

- [`runningmean`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/broman/refman/broman.html#runningmean) -
  For a set of values at a given set of positions, this calculates a
  running mean, sum, or median, with a fixed window width.

- [`runningratio`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/broman/refman/broman.html#runningratio) -
  Like `runningmean` but with a pair of values at each position, and
  then we calculate the ratio of the sums within fixed windows.

- [`runningratio2`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/broman/refman/broman.html#runningratio2) -
  Like `runningratio2` but instead of a fixed window, it adapts the
  size of the window to achieve some minimum number of points before
  taking the ratio.
- [`rmvn`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/broman/refman/broman.html#rmvn) -
  Simulate data from a multivariate normal distribution.

### Data diagnostics/cleaning

- [`align_vectors`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/broman/refman/broman.html#align_vectors)
- [`pick_more_precise`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/broman/refman/broman.html#pick_more_precise)
- [`get_precision`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/broman/refman/broman.html#get_precision)
- [`compare_rows`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/broman/refman/broman.html#compare_rows)
- [`winsorize`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/broman/refman/broman.html#winsorize)
- [`normalize`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/broman/refman/broman.html#normalize)


### System/file helpers

- [`h`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/broman/refman/broman.html#h) -
  open the help document for a function/object in your web
  browser. I'm usually using R within emacs, and if I type `help(h)`,
  the help for the `h` function shows up within emacs. So I use `h(h)`
  to see it within my web browser.
- [`make`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/broman/refman/broman.html#make)
- [`exit`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/broman/refman/broman.html#exit)
- [`objectsizes`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/broman/refman/broman.html#objectsizes)
- [`openfile`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/broman/refman/broman.html#openfile)


### Programming

- [`qr2`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/broman/refman/broman.html#qr2) -
  the base function `qr` does a QR decomposition, but the results it
  produces are not what you might expect. This function gets you the
  basic Q and R pieces.
- [`simp`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/broman/refman/broman.html#simp) -
  Perform numerical integration by Simpson's rule.
- [`trap`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/broman/refman/broman.html#trap) -
  Perform numerical integration by the trapezoidal role.


### Programming helpers

- [`%nin%`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/broman/refman/broman.html#+25nin+25),
  [`%win`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/broman/refman/broman.html#+25nin+25), and
  [`%wnin%`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/broman/refman/broman.html#+25nin+25`) -
  compare to the recent addition to base R, `%notin%`.
- [`attrnames`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/broman/refman/broman.html#attrnames)
- [`cf`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/broman/refman/broman.html#cf)
- [`convert2hex`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/broman/refman/broman.html#convert2hex)
- [`dec2hex`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/broman/refman/broman.html#convert2hex)
- [`fac2num`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/broman/refman/broman.html#fac2num)
- [`hex2dec`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/broman/refman/broman.html#hex2dec)
- [`lenuniq`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/broman/refman/broman.html#lenuniq)
- [`maxabs`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/broman/refman/broman.html#maxabs)
- [`paste00`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/broman/refman/broman.html#paste00)
- [`paste.`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/broman/refman/broman.html#paste%2E")
- [`setRNGparallel`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/broman/refman/broman.html#setRNGparallel)
- [`unsetRNGparallel`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/broman/refman/broman.html#setRNGparallel)
- [`switchv`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/broman/refman/broman.html#switchv)

### Data visualization

- [`ciplot`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/broman/refman/broman.html#ciplot)
- [`dotplot`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/broman/refman/broman.html#dotplot)
- [`grayplot`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/broman/refman/broman.html#grayplot)
- [`grayplot_na`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/broman/refman/broman.html#grayplot_na)
- [`manyboxplot`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/broman/refman/broman.html#manyboxplot)
- [`mypairs`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/broman/refman/broman.html#mypairs)
- [`qqline2`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/broman/refman/broman.html#qqline2)
- [`timeplot`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/broman/refman/broman.html#timeplot)
- [`excel_fig`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/broman/refman/broman.html#excel_fig)
- [`venn`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/broman/refman/broman.html#venn)
- [`histlines`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/broman/refman/broman.html#histlines)

### A particular data visualation: ternery diagram

- [`triplot`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/broman/refman/broman.html#triplot)
- [`tripoints`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/broman/refman/broman.html#tripoints)
- [`trilines`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/broman/refman/broman.html#trilines)
- [`triarrow`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/broman/refman/broman.html#triarrow)
- [`tritext`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/broman/refman/broman.html#tritext)
- [`trigrid`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/broman/refman/broman.html#trigrid)


### Data visualization helpers

- [`arrowlocator`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/broman/refman/broman.html#arrowlocator)
- [`colwalpha`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/broman/refman/broman.html#colwalpha)
- [`brocolors`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/broman/refman/broman.html#brocolors)
- [`crayons`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/broman/refman/broman.html#crayons)
- [`plot_crayons`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/broman/refman/broman.html#plot_crayons)
- [`jiggle`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/broman/refman/broman.html#jiggle)
- [`revgray`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/broman/refman/broman.html#revgray)
- [`revrainbow`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/broman/refman/broman.html#revrainbow)
- [`theme_karl`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/broman/refman/broman.html#theme_karl)
- [`time_axis`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/broman/refman/broman.html#time_axis)
- [`twocolorpal`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/broman/refman/broman.html#twocolorpal)
- [`xlimlabel`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/broman/refman/broman.html#xlimlabel)
- [`strwidth2lines`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/broman/refman/broman.html#strwidth2lines)
- [`strwidth2xlim`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/broman/refman/broman.html#strwidth2xlim)



---

[![CC BY](https://i.creativecommons.org/l/by/3.0/88x31.png)](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/3.0/)
&nbsp; &nbsp; [Karl Broman](https://kbroman.org/)
